arguments.task: Open the official DataForSEO documentation and read pages that establish: what the product/API does, documented authentication methods, API protocol/surface, any rate limit notes, and whether there is a public MCP server page. Then open official DataForSEO pricing or API pricing pages to determine self-serve vs paid/trial/gated access. Return the exact canonical URLs visited and concise extracted evidence for each field. Prefer top-level docs/pricing/auth pages that render fully in browser and avoid search snippets.

input.thought: Use browser to inspect official DataForSEO docs and pricing pages directly for structured evidence.

raw_result.description: SEO and digital marketing data API providing SERP, keyword, backlink, on-page, merchant, business, and related datasets for software and automation use cases.
raw_result.auth_notes: The official authentication page documents HTTP Basic Authentication using a Base64-encoded login:password pair in the Authorization header.
raw_result.gating_reason: The official pricing page presents self-serve pay-as-you-go access with dashboard budget controls and a visible "Try for free" option; it also notes a $50 minimum payment for paid usage.

api_surface.breadth: Public HTTP API documented under /v3 with many SEO and marketing datasets and tasks; docs state HTTP protocol with responses available in JSON by default, plus XML and HTML formats.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official first-party MCP server page or documentation was established from the gathered evidence, so this is recorded as not evidenced by first-party docs in this pass.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Rate-limit details were not conclusively captured from a first-party page in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs are extensive and productized; pricing is transparent pay-as-you-go with free trial language and minimum prepaid amount, which is favorable for initial toolkit development.
raw_result.human_review_reason: A structured field remains insufficiently supported by captured first-party evidence: mcp_exists was inferred from absence of found documentation rather than a definitive official statement, and rate-limit specifics were not conclusively captured in this pass.

result.description: SEO and digital marketing data API providing SERP, keyword, backlink, on-page, merchant, business, and related datasets for software and automation use cases.
result.auth_notes: The official authentication page documents HTTP Basic Authentication using a Base64-encoded login:password pair in the Authorization header.
result.gating_reason: The official pricing page presents self-serve pay-as-you-go access with dashboard budget controls and a visible "Try for free" option; it also notes a $50 minimum payment for paid usage.

api_surface.breadth: Public HTTP API documented under /v3 with many SEO and marketing datasets and tasks; docs state HTTP protocol with responses available in JSON by default, plus XML and HTML formats.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official first-party MCP server page or documentation was established from the gathered evidence, so this is recorded as not evidenced by first-party docs in this pass.

result.rate_limit_notes: Rate-limit details were not conclusively captured from a first-party page in the gathered evidence.
result.notable_signals: Official docs are extensive and productized; pricing is transparent pay-as-you-go with free trial language and minimum prepaid amount, which is favorable for initial toolkit development.
